passages.name: Opening
passages.content:
"So, it's a deal?"

The pile of coins glints, even in the dim, smoky light of the tavern anteroom. It's easily two, maybe even three months' worth of pay.

This place must be desperate.

Despite trying to maintain a cold expression, you can feel a slight smile force its way across your face. The man sitting across from you - the magistrate of this town - stares with hope in his eyes.

"Deal," you grunt matter-of-factly.

The man exhales a breath he must have been holding for at least the last two minutes. He's older, but still strong and stocky looking, a short and unkempt beard on his chin.

"We were afraid it wouldn't be enough," he says, pushing the pile of coins towards you. "Ridinghood, our village, has been a peaceful little town for generations. This Wytch has cursed us all. Livestock missing, crops withering, marriages falling apart. We're at our wits end. When we heard the famous 'Blackwolf' was coming to town we gathered everything we could spare. You're our last hope!"

You let the smile linger for a moment before finishing the last swig of your drink. "People don't pay me for hope, they pay for results. I'll rid your town of this sorceress."

The man thanks you profusely and starts making his way out. Before he leaves, you call after him once more. "By the way, what was it you called her again? The red Wytch?"

His eyes dart around carefully, as if even saying the name summons ill fortune, "Uh, the Scarlet Wytch. On account of her long red hair."

With that he leaves you alone with a pile of gold and a mission to hunt a Wytch. You smirk to yourself at the name.

"The Scarlet Wytch of Ridinghood."

Leave

The birds and insects chirp merrily as you make your way up the steep mountain path. The scenic green trees against a blue sky make it hard to remember that dark sorcery dwells here. Knowing the danger, you don't let the serene sights distract you. You place every step carefully, aware of each snapped twig or rustle in the bushes - ready for an ambush at any moment...

Thankfully, nothing happens. Before long you find yourself at a clearing around the Wytch's cabin. It's small, and in good condition, despite the distance from town. Truth be told, you're not sure whether to feel relieved or worried by how average it is.

passages.name: DinnerFirst
passages.content: Soon enough, it's evening, and time for dinner. She heads to the cauldron, haphazardly dumping a basketful of raw meat and vegetables in. Confused, you watch as she leans down and whispers something into the pot. That same unearthly wind blows around the room and swirls over the pot. It shakes violently as the contents inside jump and convulse. In a few short moments, the whole cabin is filled with the delicious smell of warm stew. Despite a dozen demonstrations in the past day, you're still amazed at her magic - it even makes food. "Yes, I can even make stew," she says with a smile. You're not sure if she is reading your mind, or if your flabbergasted expression was obvious enough - probably the latter. She motions to a couple of bowls on the table, "Would you mind?" She plops down with a bounce at the table, as you serve portions for the both of you. "So, how was your first day?" she asks without irony through mouthfuls of dinner. She's talking as if you were a new farmhand, or a child back from their first day at school, instead of a prisoner. Curse her out Stay silent Play along

passages.name: Curse her out
passages.content: "Your soup be damned, wytch." you growl at her. For a moment, she looks genuinely hurt, before putting back on an aloof air. "You're so dramatic." You slam your fist on the table with a loud thud, "Dramatic?! You've trapped me here, you demon! Am I supposed to happily eat stew and pretend I'm not your prisoner?!" Completely unfazed, she takes her time to finish chewing. "Yes." Your fist grows tighter and your jaw clenches more, "I swear I will…" "Oh shut up." she cuts you off with a command, her spells compelling you to silence. "You will eat your stew silently." her words come slow and angry. "I didn't have to do this you know. I could have erased your mind, left you a drooling mess. I could have turned you into a frog. No, I instead chose to keep you here in hopes I can change your mind." "You call me unjust, a monster, YOU were the one who broke into my house looking to KILL me!" she slams down her spoon, and looks away, tired and upset. After a few moments, she lets out a long sigh. "I was so looking forward to fucking you half the night, but I'm too upset now. Finish up and sleep down here. You can speak again in the morning." She sulks off upstairs, leaving you alone with your thoughts and a pile of dishes. Go to sleep

passages.name: Stay silent
passages.content: You sit silently, with her eyes on you, realizing that she will wait for you to answer. You grumble out a low, "Fine." She smiles at you, "So talkative. Well, I for one had a great day. I visited the blacksmith's son. Let me tell you, he is not only good at pounding iron." She finishes with the wink of her heavily made-up lashes. You almost choke on a bite of dinner at her lack of decorum, which she finds hilarious, laughing loudly at the far end of the table. "Oh, Blackwolf, you're so easy to rattle, it's adorable!" You clench your jaw and shoot her a stare...before realizing she's absolutely right, and just did it again. This makes you even angrier inside, but you choose to choke it down, and instead focus on your stew. She smiles at your restraint, "I think this is going to work out fine." You look back up with a cocked eyebrow, "Fine?" She explains, "You see, when some airquotes hero shows up to kill one of us, we usually just erase them, or change them into a frog or a book or something." She pauses to take a mouthful of stew, then continues talking while chewing it, "But that always seemed so excessive to me, I wondered if I could try rehabilitation instead. You, Blackwolf, are my experiment." "After all," she swallows and points the spoon at you accusingly, "you came here to kill me, I think you're getting off easy." The gravity of your situation washes over you, leaving you to sheepishly eat the rest of your stew in silence. Eventually, your bowls are empty, and your bellies are full. "Ah! I do make a mean stew! Now that dinner is done my lovely Blackwolf...I think it's time for dessert." Dessert "Ah! I do make a mean stew! Please clean up down here, and come to bed when finished." With that she leaves the mess and dishes as they are and heads upstairs. You finish cleaning and head up yourself later. Clean up then go upstairs to bed

passages.name: Play along
passages.content: You cautiously examine her expression for sarcasm, but it seems a genuine question. "Not so bad, the chores weren't very hard." She lights up considerably at your reply, "See! I knew this would work out!" "Work out?" She nods, "You see, when some airquotes hero shows up to kill one of us, we usually just erase them, or change them into an animal or an object." She pauses to take a mouthful of stew, then continues talking while chewing it, "But that always seemed so excessive to me, I wondered if I could try rehabilitation instead of turning you into a frog." The gravity of your situation washes over you, as it dawns on you what could have been. She sees your expression drop, and laughs again, "Don't worry, I've never been into that frog thing. I have much more interesting kinks." Her expression changes into that sly smile and seductive eyes, "Speaking of which, I've had enough dinner, how about a little dessert?" Dessert "But that's for another day (wink). Please clean up down here, and come to bed when finished." With that she leaves the mess and dishes as they are and heads upstairs. You finish cleaning and head up yourself later. Head upstairs to bed

passages.name: DinnerFirstDecision
passages.content:
The room is nearly pitch black, save for a little moonlight creeping in through the window.

You're exhausted, but sleep is the last thing on your mind. Physically today was not difficult, but all the changes have left your body feeling used and alien. More than your tired, you are emotionally drained. Stuck here with this woman whom you barely know, as she imprisons and enslaves you...you should absolutely despise her.

And yet, she has a point. With her power, she could have ended you a dozen times over, or subjected you to fate worse than death. Not that this forgives everything else she's done...

You squeeze your eyes closed, stuck in consideration, and decide to let sleep come. Moments before drifting off, your brain wanders to a final thought...

It could be worse, and she has been kind, I will play along.

I need to play along to find an escape.

I need to fight her with all my being.

passages.name: WakeUpAlone
passages.content:
Day

You wake up at the sunrise, a bright beam of light creeping in the window and hitting you square in the face.

As rude an awakening as it is, you appreciate the warmth after a long, chilly night on the floor, surrounded by the mess you're expected to clean up again today. You sigh heavily and try to stretch out the kinks and aches of the night.

Not long after, you hear creaking on the stairs as  is finally awake and coming down, all smiles despite her anger last night.

Head Downstairs

Greet

Greet

Greet

passages.name: Dinner-Name
passages.content: Soon enough, it's evening, and time for dinner. She heads to the cauldron, haphazardly dumping a basketful of raw meat and vegetables in. Confused, you watch as she leans down and whispers something into the pot. That same unearthly wind blows around the room and swirls over the pot. It shakes violently as the contents inside jump and convulse. In a few short moments, the whole cabin is filled with the delicious smell of warm stew. Despite a dozen demonstrations in the past day, you're still amazed at her magic. You grab a couple of bowls and silverware and serve portions for each of you, while she plops down in her chair with a bounce. "I'm so hungry! So what should we talk about tonight to chew the fat?" You pause for a moment to think, and realize you basically know nothing about her. "Ok, tell me a little about yourself." A look of genuine surprise washes over her. "Me?" "Yeah, I mean, if we're going to be here together for a while, we should get to know each other.." Her surprise melts into a soft smile. "Wow, , that's surprisingly sweet of you. So what is it you wanted to know?" "I suppose I should start with your real name." Her face turns a little shy, then after a short pause, "Cassandra. Cassandra Smith." You burst out laughing, "Smith?" "Hey, don't laugh! What's wrong with Smith?" "Nothing, nothing wrong...it's just, so normal. I expected something more, I don't know...magical? Like...Starloom or Emberflower or something like that." She snerks in laughter along with you, "Starloom? I'll take normal any day over that. Besides, we can't all be as blessed with nicknames as The Blackwolf." "Oh, I don't know, the Scarlet Wytch isn't bad at all." She stops laughing as her smile fades, and she stares down into her stew. "No, I suppose not." You're not sure why, but you can see she isn't a fan of her title. Try to Empathize Play it off

passages.name: Try to Empathize
passages.content: "I'm sorry, I didn't realize you didn't like it." "Don't worry about it, it's fine. There are far worse things for wytches to be called." You realize you don't know her past, and how hard things may have been for her...how hard they still might be. You find yourself sympathizing with her. Finally after a long while thinking, you speak up softly, "Well in that case, what would you like me to call you?" The look of joy that washes over her is immense, she smiles nearly with tears in her eyes. "Cassandra would be great." You finish dinner shortly after, and you automatically start cleaning up. She comes up behind you and wraps her arms around your waist. "Leave that for tomorrow. You deserve a little special treatment tonight." Dessert You finish dinner shortly after, and you automatically start cleaning up. You're surprised by her arms wrapping around your waist. "I just wanted to say thank you. Here I thought I was teaching you a lesson." With that she leaves the mess and dishes as they are and heads upstairs, smiling the whole time. Head upstairs to bed

passages.name: Play it off
passages.content: Seeing she's upset, you decide to try and play it off. "Hey! Don't worry about it, it's fine. There are far worse things for wytches to be called." "Yes, that's also very true," she replies darkly. Seems you've hit on a sore spot. You decide to play it safe and let dinner pass by in silence. When everything is finished, you automatically start cleaning up. She comes up behind you and starts tugging at your waistband. You turn to see that sly smile back on her face, "C'mon upstairs. I need your...help to take my mind off things. By the way, you can call me Cassandra." Dessert You finish dinner shortly after, and you automatically start cleaning up. She's calmed down enough to thank you for dinner and cleanup. While she's heading upstairs to bed, she pauses with a smile and says, "By the way, you can call me Cassandra." Finish cleaning, and head upstairs to bed

passages.name: SleepUp
passages.content:
By the time you make it upstairs, she's already in the bed snoring deeply.

Carefully you slip into the bed, as quiet as possible.

Alone with your thoughts...

Drift off to sleep

passages.name: Dinner-NameDecision
passages.content:
The room is nearly pitch black, save for a little moonlight creeping in through the window.

Another day trapped here with . But she seems to have warmed up to you a little, even given you her name. She seems to think of you more as a person than a prisoner.

As you begin to drift off, you're left wondering, do you feel the same?

Yes, she is a person, and I'll call her Cassandra from now on.

No, she's still trapping me here, and I won't show her the respect of a name.

passages.name: Enterfirst
passages.content:
You very slowly and cautiously push the door open, careful to not let it creak.

The dimly lit interior is again average for a small cabin. A table and chairs sit across from a hearth. One window, one door, and a staircase - likely leading to the bedroom. The most notable detail you see is that it's an absolute mess. Ashes piled in the hearth; clothes, books, scrolls and dishes strewn about carelessly; it looks like it hasn't had a good cleaning in weeks or months. Abandoned?

You look around carefully and silently - no easy task among the litter all over the floor. On the table are the remnants of a bowl of soup. It has a thick skin on top, but no mold, and the coals in the hearth still have some heat. Someone was here at least a day ago, if not this morning.

As you near the staircase, you hear the quiet shuffle of fabric and creak of furniture from above. Fortune smiles, you may have caught the Wytch sleeping in bed!

Go upstairs

passages.name: UpstairsFirst
passages.content: Step by step, at an agonizingly slow pace, you make your way up the stairs. Careful not to make the slightest sound. … The bedroom is small, and save for a few bookshelves and a tiny closet to the side, it is almost entirely filled by a huge, down-stuffed bed. Sprawled out on the bed is a beautiful woman, sleeping soundly. Long and lithe limbs with a perfect balance of softness and muscle. A gently curving waist that flares out to a generous set of sensual hips. The breasts are larger than average and perky enough to point upwards even laying down. She's absolutely stunning, but she's not the Wytch. For one thing, her hair is pink. And her skin is purple. And she has horns. And a massive cock. Your face skews up at the lewd figure before you, a demon. This might be the Wytch's familiar...or just a plaything contracted from dark powers. She stirs slightly with a long, sensual "Mmmmmmmm" That huge appendage between her legs twitches and begins to inflate, "Mistress, you're back earl…" As her eyes flutter open she sees you, weapon raised and ready to strike. A look of surprise flashes through her eyes, before being replaced with one of fury. "YOU WILL NOT HARM MY MISTRESS!" she shrieks at you, leaping up from the bed with inhuman speed! Honed instinct takes over. In an instant, she is on you. Her claws wrapped around your throat, perfect body pressed up against your armor. The next instant she stops, your sword having found its mark - straight through her belly. She moans again - still managing to sound wanton and lustful, even in her death throes. Flame and smoke pours out the wound and consumes her in a burst. Within seconds, it's over and the demon is banished back to her realm. You barely have time to catch your breath when you hear a sound downstairs. "Beryl! I'm home!" "Shit" Hide

passages.name: HideFirst
passages.content: You can hear her rummaging about downstairs. Followed by the sound of something breaking and a string of profanities. "Gods damn it all, we need somebody to clean this place up!" You have a few seconds to hide and prepare an ambush. Bed? Too open. Under the bed? Not enough room, bad angle. Closet? Closet. There's barely enough room for you to fit, but you manage to squeeze in, leaving only the slightest crack to see out of. Just in time too, as you can hear the other person coming up the stairs. "Beryl, have you spent the whole day in bed? You'd better at least be hard and ready for me." As soon as she enters, you know it's the Wytch. The descriptions match her perfectly. Beautiful, enchanting eyes, long scarlet hair, and a massive pair of perfect breasts. She stops in confusion at not finding her companion, just a step too far for an attack. Just a little closer… "Beryl?" her eyes scan the room, stopping on the cracked door of the closet. A bead of sweat forms on your brow. "Beryl, are you hiding in the closet? And why does the room stink of...sulfur…" A look of realization seems to cross over her, but rather than anger or fear, she smiles. "My,what a playful little game, with you refusing to come out of the closet like that." "Heh, the better to surprise you." you think to yourself. She steps forward, that smile growing playfully. You feel a breeze blowing past the closet doors against your face. Now is your chance! You burst from the closet to swing at her! Except that you don't. Your arms spasm and your sword jerks, knocking the closet open wide, but despite all your efforts, your legs will not move. "I told you you'd refuse to leave the closet, magic can be very persuasive." she stops drawing closer, but the devilish smile remains. Then her gaze drifts downward, "Dangerous looking weapon you have there." Your grip tightens around your sword, "the better to run you through with, Wytch!" "Hardly what I meant, but you make a good point." A look of concentration draws over her. "My,what an impressive sword and suit of armor you have. Too bad you forgot to bring them." Something about her words...unnatural...commanding. Not just commanding you, but as the phrase echoes in the small room, she seems to be commanding reality itself. Change

passages.name: ChangeFirst
passages.content: A cold wind blows over you, slowly growing in strength. As it blows stronger, you hear your armor buckle and creak. Suddenly, impossibly, it starts to dissolve, as if it were made of nothing but sand. The "grains" of your sword and armor are picked up in the otherworldly wind, swirling around you before disappearing entirely. In seconds, you are left in your underclothes, standing frozen before the Wytch. The blood drains from your face. She has you trapped, defenseless. For the first time in a very long time, you begin to feel real fear. "Looks like I guessed right, handsome. You are well armed." You cover your body as much as you can, your face burning in anger and embarrassment. "You'll never best me, demon!" "No, I’m sure I haven’t got a chance against you," she says, rolling her eyes. "Well, until you "best" me, whatever shall we do with you?" She sits on the edge of the bed to ponder...almost getting within arms reach. "You need some kind of punishment. You broke into my house, tried to kill me, and banished my lover. Do you know how hard they are to summon? I don't even know where I put her phallactary." "The world is better off with one less succubus monster." "Oh she wasn't hurting anybody! Aside from fucking, she spent most of her time lounging in bed. Barely cooked and never cleaned...this place is a complete mess and I..." she pauses mid-sentence, a smile returning. "I think I've decided your punishment." She stands up again, looking somehow imperious in her decision, "My,what a kind, obedient servant you are. Always trying to please your mistress. And you would never try to harm her." That feeling of a cold breeze again blows past your face, raising goose-bumps and ruffling your hair. "What's your name?" You don’t want to tell her, but the words fall out regardless, "Jacob, they call me the Blackwolf." She snickers a little, "Alright, then Blackwolf. I think that's enough excitement for tonight. You may sleep downstairs, and you may not leave this place unless I say otherwise. Understood?" Your mind, your heart, every fiber of your being screams to attack, to launch and tackle the bitch, end her dark magic. Your muscles flex and strain, sweat forms at your brow, you open your mouth to yell a war cry…but all that comes out is, "Yes mistress." Obey

passages.name: FirstNight
passages.content:
You descend into the darkened downstairs, trying hard not to trip on the clothes and clutter all over the floor. Unable to disobey.

Dejected and exhausted, you find a stool and sit. Alone and terrified in the dark.

Next Morning

passages.name: MorningFirst
passages.content: The sun creeps in through the small window, hitting your tired eyes and making you squint. You didn't sleep. You tried to escape, dozens of times. You searched the place vainly over and over for something, some way to break the spell and escape. Nothing. By the morning you're exhausted, ragged looking, and somehow even angrier than before. "Rough night?" a sing-song voice drifts down from the stairs. The Wytch walks down the stairs, her voluptuous, sexy body bouncing and swaying in every direction at once. She's wearing some kind of negligee nightdress, the sheer fabric providing little support and even less modesty. You stare at the lurid display provided by her simply walking, getting turned on in spite of your burning anger. Without the slightest hint of shame, she strips off the skimpy nightgown, giving an even better view of her creamy skin bulging out of her underclothes. She smiles, purposefully and obviously trying to tempt you. It's working, your traitorous cock stiffening even more in your underwear. She definitely notices, "Mmm, save that for tonight, stud, you have other chores to attend to first." She leans against the table, crossing her arms under her copious chest. "So here's the deal, Jacob the Blackwolf. I have decided on the punishment for your transgressions. You will be my servant. I expect you to handle the chores around the house. You'll receive a list each day before I go out. If you do them well, you will be rewarded, if not, you will receive more punishment." You remain stone faced and angry. "How long?" "What?" "How long will this punishment last?" "Hmmm, good question. I guess until I feel you've redeemed yourself or are no longer a threat to me." "No longer a threat?" She smiles broadly, "Exactly. I think you're too used to being a big, powerful, warrior, and you're too dangerous to the magical world. Unless you serve me well and learn your lesson, I'm going to change you. To be less of a threat...and in ways that I find most pleasing." "Do your worst, wytch." "Oh no, Blackwolf, I'm going to do my best!" You laugh genuinely at her jokes, empathize with her stories, tear up at her troubles...it almost feels like she's an old friend you grew up with, or maybe even something more? Enjoy it. Use it Fight it

You're making each other happy, and right now at least, where you are feels much more important than how you got here. Dinner is over in the blink of an eye, and before you know it, it's nearly time to go to bed. ...though nowhere near time to sleep, she tells you with a seductive wink. passages.content: Soon enough, it's evening, and time for dinner. You grab a couple of bowls and silverware and serve portions for each of you, while she sits down at the far end of the table. "Thank you" she says, coldly and matter-of-factly as you pass her the bowl. She doesn't wait for you to sit before she starts eating, pulling a scroll off the bookshelf and reading it to herself. You sit in the uncomfortable silence, the only sounds are the crackling of the fire and occasional slurp of soup. Try to make amends Say nothing Confront her

passages.name: DinnerDefault-Terrible
passages.content: She sits at the table, signifying she's ready for dinner. You grab a bowl of hot stew and set it down in front of her. She doesn't thank you...or even look at you for that matter, just turning to read a nearby book. Even after finishing her soup, she still continues reading for some time. Letting you stand there, the only sound your rumbling stomach. She's not angry or even disappointed anymore, she's just barely acknowledging you. Like you're no longer even a person to her. You have to admit to being a little worried by this treatment, but a part of you is also relieved that she's stopped trying to convert you to whatever evil plan she had. Serve her well Do the minimum

passages.name: Dinner-GoodJob
passages.content: Soon enough, it's evening, and time for dinner. It's a mostly uneventful, though quite pleasant, dinner. You and trade the events of your days and a few stories of your younger years. Afterward, while cleaning up the bowls, you suddenly feel a pair of arms wrap around you from behind! has snuck up behind you for a surprise hug! She squeezes you almost painfully tight - reminding you once again of how small and delicate you are nowShe squeezes you tightly for a few moments before letting go again with a satisfied sigh. "Dare I ask what that was for?" She leans against the nearby bookshelf, all smiles, "I know you don't always get it right, but you've been doing an excellent job, I just wanted to give you a nice thank you." "You know, a great way to thank me would be letting me go." "Thank you, it...hasn't been all bad." Pull away, "I don't need your thanks."

passages.name: Dinner-HowsTheStew
passages.content: Soon enough, it's evening, and time for dinner. You serve out the dinner and sit to eat. After the first few bites she speaks up, "So…how’s my stew?" In truth, it’s not bad. You prefer a little heavier spicing all around, but it’s as good as you often get on the trail, and leagues better than iron rations or the like. "Good" "Could use more salt" "Fine"

passages.name: Dinner-Hunted
passages.content: Soon enough, it's evening, and time for dinner. You grab a couple of bowls and silverware, talking and laughing with even before you've even sat down to eat. Eventually, curiosity gets the better of you and you ask her about herself. "How long have you been living here?" "I think about two or three years now." That's a surprise, "Really? The townspeople said it was only a matter of months." "Yes, it took this town a while to notice me." "How did you keep it secret for so long? You'd think such a small town would notice all the missing people and animals." "Missing people...missing animals? What are you talking about?" "All the people and livestock you, uh," you realize mid-sentence you don't actually know which rumors you've heard about wytches are actually true. "Um...that you abduct?" She frowns at you, looking more hurt than upset. "What do you think I am, some kind of troll? I don't abduct animals and much less people!" You look down, realizing how foolish you must look. "Sorry, the townsfolk said that…" Anger flashes in her eyes, "Townsfolk say a lot of things! They do a lot of things! That's why I've only been here for a couple of years. That's why I have to move every time townsfolk find out about me. They come with pitchforks and torches, or worse hunters and mercenaries." She's obviously upset, punctuating that last point with a glare at you. You think about it a moment, as the weight of your profession comes to bear on you. You'd never really thought about the lives of the creatures your hunt. Your voice is soft and shaky with the size of the realization, "That sounds horrible...terrible. I'm...I'm sorry." Her anger vanishes, and she looks down. You recognize the shakiness in her voice as trying to hold back tears. "It is. For all the power and wonder magic brings, it has a price. Few tolerate us, and even fewer trust us. It's a very lonely life." She can't seem to bring herself to look at you, instead staring into the fireplace. You wonder, is that the real reason you're here? Is she keeping you as punishment, or is she actually just desperately lonely? You try to find words, "I…" "Don't say it. Just...just leave me be. I'll be fine in the morning." Do as she asks, leave her be Embrace her

passages.name: Dinner-BadJob
passages.content: Soon enough, it's evening, and time for dinner. You grab a couple of bowls and silverware and serve portions for each of you, while she sits down at the far end of the table. "Thank you, at least you did that." she says, sarcasm dripping from her voice. "Did I not meet your expectations for a prisoner?" you say, trying to add your own helping of sarcasm. She's not dissuaded, "As a matter of fact, no. I'm trying to teach you a lesson here, and you're obviously not learning. If things don't improve, I'll have to find another punishment." The way she says that last sentence sounds particularly dark. You get the feeling that whatever the other punishment is, it will not be pleasant. Apologize Agree to try harder Refuse

passages.name: Try to make amends
passages.content: "Hey um…" your breath catches in your throat. It's surprisingly hard to swallow your pride. "Yeeees?" "I'm sorry, I should have done better today. I'll try harder tomorrow." She keeps her tone cool and steady, you're not sure if she's still angry or just acting the part, "Well...that's a good start. If you improve, I suppose we can make things work." The rest of the meal passes mostly quietly, but with a much lighter air. Eventually, she finishes and gives a long stretch. "I'm glad you decided to make up." "Oh, why's that?" "Because , I'm horny as fuck." With that she grabs your hand and practically drags you up to the bedroom. Upstairs The rest of the meal passes mostly quietly, but with a much lighter air. Eventually, she finishes and gives a long stretch. "I suppose that's enough heavy tension for one evening, I'm off to bed. I'll hold you to your promise." She gives a cursory "Goodnight" before going upstairs. You finish cleaning and head up yourself later. Head upstairs to bed

passages.name: Confront her
passages.content: Annoyed with her constant peeping, you decide to confront her about it. "What! What do you keep watching me for?!?" you spit out angrily. "Gods , you are such a little drama queenso emotional." Of course, this redoubles your anger, you feel your face turn red. You suck in a deep breath to scream your rage at her, "You…" "Stop." A simple word, and her magic flows around you. You're stuck, frozen in place, shaking with rage. on the other hand looks completely calm, still looking at her scroll. All your rage and indignation is less trouble than a fly to her. "You're nothing more than a ball of rage. I wonder if there's any help for you afterall." She calmly rolls up her scroll and prepares herself for bed. "You can move now, but sleep down here tonight. We can see if you're in a better mood in the morning." With that, you're left alone with your anger and a slowly dying fire. Go to sleep

Really? Did she just say she might let you go soon? You perk up immediately at the idea. Noticing this, she laughs again. "Well if that didn't get you all excited? Let's go upstairs and share some of that excitement!" Upstairs "Oh you are so cute! Looking all excited!" she pinches your cheek and gives you a kiss goodnight before heading upstairs. Upstairs You hear her satisfied voice from behind you, "That makes me very happy to hear, ." She lets you go and you two proceed to have a very pleasant dinner and evening. passages.name: Ignore her
passages.content: You ignore her, not willing to comply with even the smallest request. She stands up again, slamming her hand on the table. You pull out a bowl and set it in front of her, but you keep your eyes defiant and neglect to add any soup. This sets her off even more, "I don't know what to do with you, ! You will do everything I ask without complaint, and from now on, you will only refer to me as Mistress." You feel her magic wash over you again. Nothing changes physically, but you know all too well what she's done. You shudder internally - not sure if it's from the magic or from realizing what's just happened. She takes her time with it, but the bowl eventually dwindles down to the bottom. She stands up and finally looks at you again, her voice calm but icy cold. "You may eat, then clean the dishes. You sleep down here from now on." With that, she turns and heads upstairs. You hear the bedroom door close and are left with an empty room, a worried feeling, and a cold bowl of soup. Go to sleep

You keep your head down, genuinely feeling shame. really did treat you well, you feel sorry for disappointing her. "It's just...so much to cope with, all these changes, all these chores, I don't even know who I am anymore." 's upset expression completely melts. She coos in sympathy, "Awwww…! passages.name: Book
passages.content:
You look around the bookshelves for a good place to start learning about Wytches and magic, eventually finding the perfect one. A huge, heavy book, bound in purple-dyed leather and gold inlay.

"A Wytch's Introduction to Magic"

You doubt there could be a better place to start. The book creaks open and starts - rather appropriately - at the beginning of the world, with the wytch myth of creation.

Apparently, Wytches believe that in the beginning, there were two brothers, Adam and Steve, alone in an empty void.

Steve had the power to create anything he wished, completely out of nothing. Just by singing a song, he created mountains, rivers, trees, animals, everything we know. Steve essentially created the world.

Adam had the power of change. He would take Steve's creations and speak them into a dazzling array of different colors, shapes, and sizes. He is responsible for the beauty and variety of plants and animals.

The book also mentions how the brothers loved their creation, and describes graphically, and at length, how they loved each other. It's surprisingly erotic.

By the end of the chapter, after reading about the two brothers "adventures", you're flushed and red-faced. You'll have to read more later…for, uh, studying purposes. You close the book in a hurry and get back to work.

As you finish, you hear the telltale sound of footsteps coming up the path. In a panicked rush you return the book and head downstairs.

Downstairs

Finish reading

Finish reading

You continue reading the book, lost in the mysterious tale Adam and Steve….

It continues by describing how the brothers spent millennia travelling the world, creating new lands and new life. A paradise, wondrous to behold.

"But it would not last," the book explains, "as time wore on, Adam began to become bored with his power. He wasn't content just modifying the things Steve had made, and wished to be able to create on his own.

Boredom led to jealousy, which led to hatred. Soon Adam began making malicious changes to Steve's creation. Pitting animals against one another by making some predator and prey; turning enticing berries poisonous; making some creatures strong and dominant, but others weak and submissive."

The chapter ends with Adam's ultimate betrayal, and turning his jealous eyes on his own brother Steve…

Looking up, you notice the sunbeams have drifted quite a bit across the room. The book was so interesting, time completely escaped you. "I'll have to read more later," you think while close the book in a hurry to get back to work.

As you finish, you hear the telltale sound of footsteps coming up the path. In a panicked rush you return the book and head downstairs.

Downstairs

You left off with Adam approaching Steve, ready to change his own brother out of envy.

"Adam spoke Steve's form into something to soothe his burning jealousy. Something he could dominate and possess. Something smaller and softer, with soft curved lines where straight angles once were. They aren't watching you, they're watching you." You're completely shocked, stunned by the news. "I'm…hot? You mean they're all just…" "Yup, probably trying to imagine you naked." "I never even considered it. I mean, I know what I look like, and I know that I would probably take a glance. But I didn't really expect it to be this bad, this obvious." "You don't know the half of it, girl. Men are dogs like that, they can't resist a pretty face and a tight little body. Or a nice rack, or a sweet ass - and girl, you're ranking pretty high on those criteria. Hell, I bet you could walk up to nearly any boy in town and have your way with him." goes back to eating nonchalantly while you sit in stunned silence. You're a hottie. The implications swirl around your conflicted mind. Is this good? It's not exactly bad. Should you be happy? Angry? Could you really go out and pick up random guys? "You know, you really ought to eat your dinner. You're going to need your strength if you want to catch a man next time you're in town." "Though, you don't have to wait that long if you don't want to." Upstairs She eventually finishes and goes upstairs, leaving you to your thoughts…and a lot of dishes.. Head upstairs to bed You can now use your looks to pick up random people for sex when in town.

passages.name: HunterGlimpse
passages.content: As you walk through the streets, you happen to catch a glance at a familiar sight walking in the opposite direction. The tell-tale black armor; scarred appearance; dangling weapons and tools for all manner of beasts; oh gods, the swagger. You'd recognize another hunter anywhere. He gives you a leer and a wink as he passes by, turning and heading into the tavern.You feel the blood drain from your face as you watch him walk by and head into the inn and tavern. "Oh shit, this is bad" Next

passages.name: DinnerBroomstick
passages.content: You serve out the bowls and utensils as usual, filling each with stew for dinner as finishes changing into her house clothes and relaxes. "Oh ! You wouldn't believe the day I've had." She puts her feet up on the bench and leans back, rubbing the bridge of her nose. "Oh?" you mutter, knowing full well she's about to tell you whether you want to hear it or not. "Yes! So I met with a faery out in the woods today. She tried like three times to enchant me! I even told her I was a wytch and she didn't believe me! Said I didn't look like one! As if she expected me to fly down on a broom cackling with a pointy hat! The nerve of some people!" You chuckle nervously at her story, your experience with the broom flashing hotly in your mind. But was she being serious about it or not? Only one way to find out. "I'm kind of embarrassed to ask, but...is it true?" She eyes you suspiciously, "Is what true?" Your cheeks are getting hot again, "The...uh...broom thing? Do wytches actually ride them?" Her eyes narrow as she stares at you, studying your face. Does she know about you and the broom? You can feel your cheeks blushing more and more as her eyes burrow into you. Finally, she bursts out laughing, "Could your face get any redder ? Gods you're so cute when you're being shy!" You feel an instant wave of relief knowing that your secret is safe. "It's not a crazy question, but no, wytches don't really fly around on broomsticks. If you need to fly around for some reason, you could enchant something to fly, but a broomstick would be a lousy choice. Way too easy to fall off, I'd think. Worse, it's just too conspicuous. No better way to advertise there's a wytch nearby than for a dozen farmers to see her zoom by on a sweeper!" "That makes sense, but then why are wytches famous for riding them?" Her lips curl in a playful smile, "Because, my curious little red fox, wytches don't ride broomsticks, they ride broomsticks." You feel your face starting to turn red again as she explains. "Think about it, a broomstick is about the right size and shape, nice and smooth, but also a bit bumpy. One of the first spells a young wytch learns is how to animate objects. Are you telling me a horny little firecracker like you hasn't at least thought of that?" You shake your head a little too hard and give a very unconvincing, "No!" She side-eyes you, keeping her smile, "Uh huh. Anyway, I'm sure some time, somewhere, a wytch or two has been seen "cleaning," and the rumors probably just spread from there." Admit what you did with the broom Hide the truth!

passages.name: BroomstickBoom
passages.content: You chuckle nervously, feeling your cheeks reddening slightly, "Huh. That makes sense." doesn't answer, she just keeps watching you with that smile. Does she suspect? You feel yourself blushing even more. "I mean, I can see that…that it would…you know…fit…heh." She's still smiling. Oh gods, she knows! How could she not, your face is burning up in embarrassment! "OK FINE! You caught me! I fucked the broom! And before you ask, yes it felt fantastic! Is that what you wanted to hear?" She keeps smiling and nods, keeping her composure, "Oh? Well that sounds nice." Ok that's not the answer you were expecting. Was she expecting that? How can she remain so composed? You sit in utter shock for a moment…at least until the snickering begins. practically snorts into her bowl, unable to hold back the laughter anymore. You also start laughing so hard that you're both gasping for air. "Oh Red, welcome to the club!" Eventually you stop laughing and it's nearly time to go to bed. "C'mon upstairs Red, let's have a little fun. Feel free to bring the broom if you like." "Ha ha, funny joke." "That was a joke, right?" Upstairs You finish cleaning and head up yourself later. Head upstairs to bed

passages.name: DinnerMonsters
passages.content: You serve out dinner and sit down together to eat. She strikes up the conversation, "So I've told you all about myself and my magic, how about you?" You shrug, "Um, ok. What do you want to know?" "Well…what's a hunter's life like? I know! What's the biggest monster you've ever fought." It's your turn to smile, you enjoy telling stories of your hunts. "That's not the question you want to ask," you say dramatically, "You want the second biggest. It's a much better story." "Oooh, tell me about it!" She leans in on her elbows, eyes sparkling with anticipation. "The biggest was a gorgeworm that got way too large for its own good. Big enough to wrap itself around this house..twice! But it was easy to take care of, winter was coming, so I just waited for it to go into hibernation and put a spear through its head." You stab a carrot with your fork for emphasis. "Wow! Still brave of you, if it woke up, it could have eaten you whole!" You shrug, "They're heavy sleepers. Like I said, the worm wasn't that interesting. The second biggest, and nearly the size of a house itself, was an ogre!" gasps, covering her mouth, "An ogre! I take it back, you’re not brave, you’re crazy! Even with magic I wouldn't mess with an ogre! They’ll eat an entire village in a night! Guards and all!" "Oh I know! In fact, this ogre had done just that. By the time I'd arrived, the town was empty, either deserted or just dessert. When I got there, he'd crushed in a thatch roof cottage, and was using the whole house like a makeshift chair while he finished eating a flock of sheep." "Did you wait for him to sleep too? Stab him in the head?" she mimics the action, fully engrossed in the tale. You laugh, "Not this time. Ogres aren't only huge, they're hard to kill. Arms and legs are strong and wide, their bellies are huge and fat, and their skulls are thick with bone. There’s nowhere within reach where you can actually injure them. The only real vulnerable parts are their eyes or throat. But with this one sitting on a crushed house, there was no way to get up there sneakily for an attack." "What did you do?" "The only sensible thing, I threw a tomato at him." "What?" "Yup - Splat! Right across his forehead." "What the heck would that do, besides make him angry?" "That's the point, I wanted him to chase me. And he did! He was up and roaring in a flash! I could feel the ground shake with every footfall as he ran towards me. Thankfully, I made my way to the inn." "Wait, you said this thing was as big as a house, and even crushed one to make a chair. How would going into an inn protect you? Wouldn't he just crush it too?" "That’s what I was counting on. See, before I threw the tomato, I snuck around town a bit. This inn had a very nice, and very deep wine cellar. When the ogre tried to crush me, he went straight through the floor! I was already out the other side, ready to strike when he fell!" You pull a spoon across your throat and mine the death throes of the ogre. claps in delight. "What a story! Oh, I'm going to need more of those!" "It was a good one, hefty profit, and I found a good few unopened bottles of wine." After a short pause, "How about you?" "What about me?" "You're a part of the world of magic, you must have some fantastical run-ins." "Me? Oh no. Some wytches go for those kinds of adventures, but most stay away from that life. It's a little weird, but I can't help feel a little guilty about monsters." "What do you mean?" "Most of the monsters in the world were made by wytches." "You made them?!?" you nearly drop your spoon. Her eyes flash in anger for a moment before cooling, "No, not me! And not on purpose…usually. It's mostly inexperienced wytches who messed up their spells. Magic can be dangerous and unpredictable." "How does somebody mess up a spell and create an ogre?" "At a guess, probably a spell to make someone stronger, or taller, or both. But they overdid it, or didn’t control themselves when casting." Your eyes are wide and your jaw is nearly on the table, "And you're telling me most monsters came about this way?" "Wytch magic is very old and very powerful." "How about harpies?" "Flight spell." "Lizardfolk?" "Enchanted pet." "Gelatinous cubes?" "A fetish carried waaay too far." "Vampires?" "Ugh, trust me, you don't want to know." You sit back, absolutely stunned. Your world just got a whole lot larger and smaller at the same time. You feel the blood drain from your face as another realization comes over you, "So ogres were people." "At one point, yes, some people somewhere were turned into orges. Though now most are just born as ogres from ogre parents. Still though, kind of puts your profession in a new perspective, doesn't it?" That's an understatement, "I always thought I was protecting people from monsters." "Don't be too hard on yourself, you probably still saved quite a few people. Monsters are often…well, monsters. That ogre still ate a town. But they might not all have deserved it. It's just a little more complicated. Believe me, magic may seem like a shortcut to solve any problem, but it never makes anything simpler." You sit quietly for a long time, head still swimming with this new information. She giggles at your perplexed state, "Well, I can see I've given you a lot to think about, and it's late. I think I'll head up for the night." "...not to mention, I promised you another battle in the bedroom tonight." Upstairs She goes upstairs, leaving you to your thoughts…and a lot of dishes.. Head upstairs to bed

passages.name: DinnerMonsters-Decision
passages.content:
Your mind is still not at ease from the dinnertime discussion.

You keep thinking back to all the monsters you've hunted over the years and how some of them might have been people.

As  said, they were monsters. They were terrorizing towns…or so the townsfolk told you, but that's also what they said about . And even if they really were evil, they were also still victims…

You always considered yourself the righteous sword of justice. When situations had been weighed, the monsters found out, that's when the people sent you. You never had to think about the monsters, just kill them.

You might not have been the one passing judgment, but you always avoided questioning it. Some part of you probably knew there were questionable parts of your profession. Ignoring the morals of your actions doesn't absolve you of them.

So where does that leave you? Can you still see yourself being a hunter, even if you were to get out of here?

No. I've got a new body and a new life now, I want to explore this path.

No. Even if I got my old body back, knowing what I know now, I couldn't be a hunter anymore.

Yes…but 'hunter' might not be the right word anymore. If a target is not dangerous, or repentant, I'd like to help them instead!

Yes, while some 'monsters' might be victims, regular people are still getting hurt and even eaten by them every day. They need a protector.

passages.name: Sketch
passages.content:
You spend a little time sketching out your magic practice partner: the broom.

For such a simple shape, you have a surprisingly difficult time drawing it out - getting all the lines and bristles to look good - and accurate.

You decide to sketch out  for some drawing practice. After all, she's constantly on your mind, and an easy visual reference. Not to mention that you see her every day - in excessive detail.

You start with her face, carefully sketching every detail as you see it in your mind. The curves of her eyes and lips, her cute nose, and her immaculately shaped eyebrows. You even surprise yourself with how well you're able to recall and draw out her face. Maybe this practice is working!

You even allow yourself some time to sketch further down her body. Her slender neck and shoulders, and of course her surging breasts - though looking it over, you may have taken a little artistic license with them.

When you pause to check your work, you're surprised to see how much time has passed. You really got into drawing her!

You're not really in the mood to carefully sketch something for practice - can't find the inspiration. Instead, you just let yourself go and draw a caricature of .

It's not much more than a few crudely drawn, smirking stick figures with long hair and big circle boobs. It's more silly than malicious, but you have a good giggle at the result - it definitely helps you relieve some stress.

You start off drawing another picture of , but don't really feel like just copying reality…after all, you're supposed to be practicing your imagination, so why not imagine?

You try drawing different versions of  - short hair, bigger boobs, a different nose…etc. It's surprisingly fun, you even understand a little more about the entertainment she gets out of changing you.

You keep going, drawing some wild stuff.  with a tail or a beak. You even draw a "cat" version of her - a small, cute, burgundy cat with violet eyes.

You like it so much, you even draw a few more versions of " cat".

You giggle to yourself at the silliness of your drawings, a small part of you wondering just how far you can go with magic once you learn more.

Right as you finish, you hear the telltale sound of footsteps coming up the path. You spent so long drawing that  is already home!

Back

Back

passages.name: LocketResult
passages.content:
Over the time you've been here, you've changed a lot, in ways obvious and some less so.  gave you a chance to re-examine…no…to re-make your entire life. And you’re happy with the result, happy being with her.

You decide right there that it’s time to tell her how you feel, and you have just the idea how!

Jumping up, you run to the bookshelf to fetch the small silver locket you found a few days ago. You pop it open, in the left half is the tiny painting of 's matriarch, Sophie, however, the right half is empty - for now. You grab a knife and get to work, cutting out an appropriate-sized picture of yourself from the sketchbook.

After a little trimming and positioning, you have it carefully set in the frame - a locket with Sophie on one side and you on the other.

You smile at the result, a feeling of happiness and warmth coming over you as you realize how big of a step this is. You can only hope  likes it!

Close Book

Congratulations! You have unlocked the "Locket " special ending! Giving the locket to  will end the game.

And the answer is no.

It's not that you hate the idea, you've seen the best qualities of , and you realize that getting closer to her would be an adventure full of passion and excitement - but it wouldn't be honest.

As you look over the paintings, you can see how much she cares about you, but it's something you just don't share…at least not now.

You sigh heavily and close the book, deciding to put these pictures and emotions away for today.

Close Book

passages.content: You grab a couple of bowls and silverware, hurrying to get dinner ready. watches you silently, reclining in her chair as if deep in thought. You feel your face going red from embarrassment, but manage to croak, "Yes?" She smiles, "It's nothing to be embarrassed about. We all have temptations and urges." You're not sure where she's going with this, but nod along. "Thanks for understanding, I guess." She continues, "I get it, you're anxious, frustrated with being here, and what's more, you've got looks that make men line up to talk to you. The temptation to throw off your responsibilities and give in to desire is simply overwhelming." You feel like a teenager getting a lecture from a parent, despite the fact that you're actually older than she is… "And the truth is, this helps me out as well. You come back, dripping in magic, saving me the need to go and recharge. So I've decided to make you an offer." Finally, the point. "For each person's worth of magic you bring me, I will forgive one chore for the day." "Wait, really? I can get out of the house, have sex, and it will get me out of work? What's the catch?" "No catch, but you'll be responsible for your end of the deal. Don't use up the magic before giving it to me. And most of all, don't make it your life to fuck random townsfolk day in and day out. You're worth more than the pleasure you can get and give." It's a deal! Sounds risky, but I agree No, I don't want that

passages.name: DinnerDoll
passages.content: You dutifully lay out the bowls of hot stew as she goes into her tale. "I was a much younger wytch then, and a lot more naive. I'd moved into a small village in some far-off province. Of course, my matron - the wytch who taught me - warned me against getting too close to villagers, but I was so confident and headstrong. I've always been good with people so I was sure I'd be the exception to how most wytches live. I thought that I could live right there, in the middle of town. That I'd be able to change their minds or win their hearts, or maybe even just blend in. As I said, young and naive. I even tried to open a little potion and medicine shop." she chuckles a little at herself, but you can see a hint of sadness in her eyes. "Anyway, one day a man rushes into my shop, begging my help. His daughter is deathly sick with fever, and he and his wife were in an absolute panic." 's eyes have dropped down, almost as if she's retelling the story to the doll instead of you. "I follow him to their home, and find their daughter is in bad shape, on death's door. I want to help, but I'd been making potions all day and didn't have enough magic left to cast any spells." "What did you do?" you ask, rapt in her story. "I did what I had to. I sucked off the father right there." You almost choke on your stew. "You what?!" Her eyes momentarily flash with anger, "it's not what I wanted to do, but I needed magic to help! Nothing more to it than that." She calms herself down with a deep breath, "Sorry, I shouldn't have snapped at you. Sex is how we wytches refill our magic. I was only trying to save the girl. "And it worked. I got my magic and saved Clara - that was her name. I got to walk around town with my head high, feeling great about myself." She pauses, looking sad again, "It lasted about three days. The man's wife found out and accused me of enchanting and bewitching her husband. He went along with her story, and before you knew it the town was blaming me for everything, including making Clara sick in the first place. "That very night, there's another unexpected knock on my door, this time it's Clara herself. She thanks me for everything and warns me that the villagers have made a mob and are coming to burn my shop - and maybe me too. So I decided to run. She gave me her doll - because it had always kept her safe and made her feel better. I owe little Clara my life for warning me that day." "I'm sorry, I thought it might be a childhood toy or something. I didn't realize it was so important to you." She sniffs and wipes at her eyes. "No…it's fine…it's actually a happy memory. It reminds me that there are some good people out there." She stands up from the table, pushing away her barely-touched stew. "That story just about did me in, I think I'll turn in a little early tonight." Before heading upstairs, she smiles down at the doll again, then looks back at you, "You know what, she's protected me for long enough, I think it's time I passed her on. You keep her, giving her a good squeeze always makes me feel better." She tosses the doll in your direction and disappears up the stairs. You look at the doll for a little while before cleaning up the dishes and heading up yourself. Head upstairs to bed

passages.name: DinnerDoll-Decision

So…now you've got a doll.

It's not an especially cute or feminine doll…but nonetheless, it feels kind of cute and feminine to just have it.

isn't trying to trick you into being more girly, is she?

Probably not, she's not that kind of tricky. And her story sounded touchingly sincere and sweet.

But if that's true, it means she's trying to support you emotionally…while changing you physically…

…which completely messes with your head.

You rub your eyes and sigh. It's too late to think about something so confusing.

The only pressing question is whether you plan to take her up on the offer and use the doll occasionally.

It can't hurt to give it a try at least.

Probably not. Only if I really need to.

No matter what I look like, I'm not a girl and I don't need a girl's toy.

passages.name: DinnerLocketIntro
passages.content: She sits down and smiles at the locket again, "It's amazing you found this, I hadn't thought about it in awhile. This was Sophie, my matriarch," she continues, opening the locket to show you the picture, "that's what we wytches call those who give us our magic. Though she was also my mentor, my friend, and much more." You study the tiny image, it's an intricately rendered painting of a regal-looking woman with piercing green eyes and bright, strawberry-red hair. Moreover, you recognize the painting style as 's from her sketchbook - she painted this. "She must have meant a lot to you." "Yes…she literally saved my life when she woke me up to magic." "Saved you? Were you a slave or a criminal or something?" "Oh no," she exclaims with a chuckle, "nothing so dramatic or cliche as that. My family was actually quite well off. Not nobility, but my father was a very wealthy merchant. I was his only daughter. I was pampered, tutored, and doted on hand and foot." You snerk at that with an eye roll, "Oh how horrible. Thank goodness you were rescued." She shoots you a glare and frown. "Yes, I am thankful. You have no idea the life of a wealthy woman. We are raised without a care in the world, without any worry or responsibility - the most comfortable hell you can imagine. How would you feel if you had no say in where you lived, what you eat, how you speak and dress, who you fuck, who you marry? Women in that world are not people, we're trophies. Would you want such a life?" You're taken aback, both by her old life and the passion she speaks about it. Moreover, you agree completely. You wouldn't want to be chained to a husband - even if the chain was made of gold. "You're right, I couldn't stand that life. I wasn't rich or pampered, grew up as a farmhand, but I couldn't let my whole world be a single farm. I first started hunting just to get away from the fields." "I thought so, from the first conversation we had, I thought you were like me. There's so much in the world to see, to taste, to feel! I hope you remember that out in the world." " You raise an eyebrow, "I'd try, but first I'd have to actually be...you know, free." "Yes yes, touche. This isn't a life sentence, you're only here as long as you need to be...or choose to be. Just as it was for me." Your eyebrow jumps up a little further in curiosity, "What does that mean?" Her usual smirk comes back, "It's getting late, let's leave it there for now. Tell you what, you keep the locket for now, as a reminder that this can all be a positive experience. Trust me on that." With that, she stretches and heads upstairs for the night. Leaving you with a mystery and a pile of dishes. Finish the dishes and head upstairs to bed

passages.name: GiveLocket
passages.content: You can't help but smile and bounce on your heels as you speak with . She cocks an eyebrow at your ear-to-ear grin, "What's got you so excited? Did you get into my potions again or something?" "Nope, I've got something for you." "For me? What do you mean." You thrust your hands forward, showing her the locket. She doesn't understand at first, "My locket, I already know about that. Thank you again for finding it." You roll your eyes at her being obtuse for once, "No...open it." Looking confused, she takes the locket from your hands and cracks it open. "Oh Red..." she stares at it with a shocked look on her face. At first, you're worried you've done something wrong. Is she mad at you cutting up her sketchbook? You try to explain yourself, "I thought you might like it, you know the two redheads in your life..." Without a word, rushes towards you with tears in her eyes, wrapping her arms around you. She's holding you so tight you can hardly breathe, your head swallowed in the deep softness of her bountiful chest. "Oh Red..." she says again through the tears, "this is the sweetest thing anyone's ever done for me!" She pulls you back long enough to let you breathe again only to shower your face with kisses before burying you again in another hug. "So I take it you like it?" She releases her hug but still holds you close, "I don't have words to describe how much I love it. Wherever did you get the idea?" "I found your sketchbook, and figured, if you have this keepsake of your matriarch, you might want one of your...well, of me." "I do. I didn't think I would ever be as close to anyone as my matriarch until I found you. You've changed me Red, maybe more than I've changed you and...I.." she leans you back again, staring deep into your eyes, "Red, I…you’ve come to mean so much to me. I can’t imagine this cabin - or my life - without you," she pauses as if summoning up the courage. "There's something I've been thinking about for some time now. Would you like to stay with me? No deal, no compulsion, just you and me, together?" Of course!

passages.name: CatSuccess
passages.content: "It worked?" You can hardly believe your eyes. Sitting on the ground in front of you is a small, burgundy, and very confused-looking cat! "It worked," you sigh in relief and collapse onto your knees on the floor. You're exhausted and drained. Not only from the spell, but emotionally as well. Your feelings about were…complicated to say the least, as was the decision to turn on her. But now that it's done, you feel a huge weight lifting off your shoulders. Almost literally, as her enchantments keeping you in the cabin evaporate off you. You feel lighter, relieved, and free again. Your internal monologue is interrupted by a brushing sensation on your leg. You'd almost forgotten about Cassandra the cat, but she's now gently rubbing your shin. "Hey there, do you remember me? Do you know who I am?" You reach down to stroke her red fur. She jumps up into your lap and nuzzles you softly. "What do I do with you now?" you wonder. "Actually, what do I do with myself now?" You’ll have to start your life over with nothing except a cat… Well, techincally - you realize looking around - you also have a cabin now... And a full wardrobe of fancy clothes and magical trinkets… And the ability to use magic - the ability to do nearly anything you want… A smile slowly spreads across your face. Later...

passages.name: CatFail
passages.content: Your spell failed…well, not exactly failed, but it didn’t quite work either. still stands in front of you, though quite different from a moment ago. She now has tiny feline whiskers, an adorable pair of cat ears, and a long tail twitching angrily behind her. "Don’t move a muscle!” she commands angrily, reasserting her magic’s control over you. You freeze instantly, unable to move or speak. ”What in the seven kingdoms did you do to me?” she looks over her changed body, feeling the tail and ears. ”Is this some kind of new kink? Answer me truthfully.” You find yourself able to speak again, the words coming out without much chance to edit them, ”I was trying to turn you into a cat, so I could escape.” ”Oh Red, really?” She actually looks a little hurt. ”After all we’ve done together, all I’ve taught you. I spared you from a fate as an animal, but you’d do that to me? I thought I knew you better…I thought I meant at least a little more to you,” as she speaks, you can see the hurt building in her eyes. Your emotions are likewise a maelstrom at the moment. You feel angry that your spell didn’t work, scared of her reprisal, and even guilt at betraying her trust and hurting her. She’s pacing back and forth now, her emotions easily as conflicted as yours. It shows on her face, as well as her surprisingly expressive ears and tail. When she finally speaks, you hear her voice wavering on the verge of tears, ”Gods Red, I thought for once, I might actually have found somebody who wouldn’t betray me. Somebody who I could share myself with. You’re worse than all the torch waving villagers put together!” You wish you could apologize - or do anything really, besides standing here looking stupid. continues pacing for ages, considering her options, sniffing back tears the entire time, ”What am I going to do now? Obviously, you can’t be trusted - so I can’t just let you leave. Especially not with magic, you’d be a danger to everyone. I suppose I could turn you into a cat, but I never wanted to do that. Besides…” She walks forward and places a hand softly on your cheek. Her eyes finally giving over to tears, ”Besides, you might not care about me, but I care about you. I’ve gotten used to you being here, Red…I actually like you. What am I going to do?” She thinks about it for a long time, sniffing through the tears. At one point, one of her pointy ears twitches. As she reaches up to scratch it, her eyes brighten somewhat. ”You know, you might have accidentally come up with the perfect solution...” Wind rushes around you as she begins casting her spell. Your vision blurs slightly as the magic takes its effect. Later...

That doesn't stop rumors from spreading about you. A mysterious, red-haired beauty who suddenly appears and seduces the townsfolk. It's funny how things have almost come full circle. When you used to arrive in town as the Blackwolf, people would stare at you, lock their doors, and whisper rumors of hunters and monsters. Now when you saunter through a village, it's nearly the same. People still stare - though it's more of a leer now, women clutch their men, and you overhear tales and warnings of wytches and seductresses mixed with whistles and innuendo.
