She avoids your face while setting out dinner. It seems you've hurt her feelings quite a bit. Eventually, after eating, excuses herself upstairs, leaving you to clean up. Just do the dishes.

passages.name: BarHunterAsk
passages.content: When the bartender eventually comes over to you, you lean across the bar to talk. In your best naive-sounding whisper you ask, "Is that one of those hunters?" He leans down close, "Sure is. They sure do stand out, don't they?" "They? Is there more than one?" "Oh, no…well…maybe? Not sure. We had one come through town a ways back. Pretty famous, you might have heard of him, the Blackwolf. He tried to help us out but…I don't think he made it." "The Blackwolf! Yes, he's famous! What happened? Did some monster eat him? Is it dangerous here?" He chuckles a bit, but puts on a reassuring tone, "not exactly. I wouldn't want to scare you, but we have a bit of a wytch problem in sleepy little Ridinghood." "A wytch! Oh my, how terrifying!" you exclaim in mock surprise. You're enjoying acting the part of a skittish young lass. "There there," he pats your shoulder for comfort, "she mostly goes after men. Nothing for a young lady like you to worry about. Besides, who knows - maybe the Blackwolf just ran off with the money." "He wouldn't do that!" you shoot back...maybe a little too quickly. The bartender raises an eyebrow for a moment before continuing, "No, I don't actually think he would, but you never know with these hunters. If it weren't for the wytch, I wouldn't want any of them in town. At least the Blackwolf had the decency to bugger off to do his job. This one barely drinks anything, just sits and takes up a table." You order a drink and try to strike up a casual-sounding conversation, "Say, what happened to that big guy in the black armor? The “hunter" was it?" The bartender rolls his eyes, "Oh him, the Ending, they call him. He's still here, up in his room." The Ending? Uh oh, you know that name. He's almost as famous as the Blackwolf. An older hunter, specializing in dealing with hauntings and magical creatures...like you. His nickname actually comes from being able to "end" those problems so well. "Oh? That's an interesting nickname." "Yeah, well I wish he'd 'end' his job already. Doing some kind of focus-ritualy-thing for days now. Not like the rest of us have to deal with a wytch problem while he dallies about. At least he's not taking up a table anymore." The Focusing Ritual! Of course! You've heard of it before, a week's worth of chastity and intense meditation to guard oneself against magic. You never put much stock into it, but now that you know magical energy is fueled by sex and imagination, it makes perfect sense. For a man, a week of meditation with no sex would probably build a reasonable defense against magic.that was before you really knew much about magic. At least you have a rough timeframe for when he's coming...but that doesn't ease the knot of fear in your stomach. You order a drink and try to strike up a casual-sounding conversation, "Say, what happened to that big guy in the black armor? The “hunter" was it?" The bartender rolls his eyes, "Oh him, the Ending, they call him. He finally decided to get to work I guess. Left already." "Oh, that's a shame," you say, having difficulty keeping from smiling as you take a sip of your drink. "Oh, that's a shame," you say worriedly. You take a sip of your drink to hide the rising fear you feel. The bartender smiles slyly back at you, "Why you asking? Fancy him?" You nearly spill your drink all over yourself in surprise, leaving the bartender laughing at his own joke. Back Leave

passages.name: HunterTalk
passages.content: You cautiously approach the table where the sits. He doesn't even look up from his book. You head upstairs to the inn's rooms. It might be the bartender's choice or just a cruel coincidence, but the has the same room as you did when you stayed here. You take a deep breath and knock softly on the door. "Um, hello, sir." you decide to make your voice sound hesitant and a little afraid. It's surprisingly easy because you're quite hesitant and a little afraid. He sighs and puts down his book. He takes his time but eventually opens the door. His eyes slide up and down your body, coming to a stop squarely on your chest.managing to look both pervy and judgmental at the same time. "Well hello there, pleasure to see a real beauty in a little hovel like this." "I'm sorry to bother you, but I heard some rumors, and I wanted to ask. Are you…a hunter?" "Aye, that I am," he smiles with a patronizing little roll of his eyes. He reaches inside one of the pockets on his belt, and pulls out a small card, all the while continuing to talk, "and while it pains me to say no to a rare beauty like yourself, please understand, I'm a very busy man." He hands you the card. It bears a small stamped sigil of the hunter's guild and a signature: "The Ending". His autograph. "There you go. Take that to keep me in mind. When I finish the job, do look me up. I know how to slay more than just monsters," he finishes with a slow, leery wink. He thought you were a fan? Worse, he thought you wanted to… Ew! Your face turns bright red as you realize what just happened. Mortified, you quickly thank him and rush out of the inn. He looks you over, sizing you up as a threat…before obviously and disappointingly deciding you aren't. "Yes?" "I'm sorry to bother you, but I heard some rumors, and I wanted to ask. Are you…a hunter?" "Aye, that I am," he smiles with a patronizing little roll of his eyes. He reaches inside one of the pockets on his belt, and pulls out a small card, all the while continuing to talk, "Listen kid, really pleased to meet you, but I hope you'll understand, I'm a very busy man." He hands you the card. It bears a small stamped sigil of the hunter's guild and a signature: "The Ending". His autograph. "There you go then. Take that, I'm sure all your friends will be very jealous." Your face turns bright red as you realize what just happened. He thought you were a fan! Mortified and nearly speechless, you quickly thank him and rush out of the inn. Leave The bartender confirms the is upstairs in his room. It might be the bartender's choice or just a cruel coincidence, but the has the same room as you did when you stayed here. You take a deep breath before knocking on the door. "Mr. hunter...um, Sir Ending??" The door opens quickly this time, causing you to jump in surprise. "Oh, you again." "I'm sorry to disturb you…" He cuts you off with a dismissive wave, "Listen , I'm flattered, really. But I'm busy now trying to cleanse and focus for my hunt. So unless you've got something that's going to help me with that, I'll wish you a good day." Cleanse? The Focusing Ritual! Of course! You've heard of it before, a week's worth of chastity and intense meditation to guard oneself against magic. You never put much stock into it, but now that you know magical energy is fueled by sex and imagination, it makes perfect sense. For a man, a week of meditation with no sex would probably build a reasonable defense against magic.that was before you really knew much about magic. At least you have a rough timeframe for when he's coming...but that doesn't ease the knot of fear in your stomach. $(function() {$("#layer8 img").attr('src', 'resources/avatar/layer8/' + State.variables.faceState + '/smirk.png')}); Just leave Tell him about "Oh, I see. Ok, I'll just…uh…go. Sorry to bother you again!" "Nah, I get it. I have that effect on the ladies. Tell you what, I'll be swinging back through town after the hunt. Look me up then and we can have a bit of fun." With that, he gives you a sly wink and closes the door, leaving you a little grossed out and alone in the hallway. He just nods gruffly and slams the door, leaving you embarrassed and alone in the hallway. Leave You smile coyly, "I'm glad you asked. That actually is the reason I came. I have some information that might help your hunt." He narrows his eyes at you before nodding and opening the door. "Ok, I'm listening." "I know the wytch. I've seen her cabin in the mountains, I've even been inside." He leans against the door frame, keeping his suspicious look. "Really? And how is it you know all this?" Lie Admit you're her prisoner Admit you're the Blackwolf "I…uh…also live in the mountains. I've seen her coming and going, we talk on occasion, I've even picked up things for her from the market. Not to mention I know a few woodsmen, and she's no stranger to them…if you know what I mean." "Seems like you know her pretty well, why turn her in to me?" "Self preservation! She hasn't done anything to me yet, but I don't know if it will last forever. Also, in case you haven't noticed, I'm also a redhead, I'd hate for a hunter like you to make a mistake and come after me instead!" A smile curls up his lips, "I'll be honest, I thought so too at first. But your eyes are the wrong color. And your tits don't match the stories." You're shocked at how direct - and insulting - he is about your chest! You’re relieved to not be under suspicion, especially as you notice a glint of light behind the door frame and realize he's been holding a sword this entire time. Looking at the polished blade, you shiver a little at the thought of how close you were to danger. He snickers a little as you stare at the sword. "Can't be too prepared. So what can you tell me about the wytch?" "I…actually I live there." He chuckles slightly and takes a step back. "Mysterious redhead with huge knockers, coming down from the mountain just to talk to me. I knew it!" as he moves, you catch the glint of a blade from behind the door! Combat instinct kicks in and you sidestep his his first lunge at you. You spin off your dodge in a fluid motion and rush forward to shove him off balance. Hopefully that will give yourself a chance to explain, or at least escape. This time however, your instincts fail you. You learned your combat moves when you were much larger and much, much stronger - you push against him with all your weight and it does nothing! It's like pushing against a stone wall! The hunter takes the advantage to grab and slam you - painfully - against the door. You cry out in desperation as he raises his sword to finish you off, "I'm her servant! Her prisoner!" He's still holding his sword up, ready to strike, but pauses, "And how do I know you're not the wytch, come here to get me first?" "I've had plenty of chances to cast a spell on you, and I didn't. And my eyes are green! Surely the villagers told you the wytch's eyes are violet, right?" He seems to think it over for a few seconds before slowly lowering his blade and releasing you. "Fair enough," he glances downwards, "in my defense though, they emphasized the tits more, and well…""now that you mention it, the tits don't match the stories either." "Yeah, thanks for reminding me," you're suddenly feeling very self-conscious, and try to cover yourself by crossing your arms. Unfazed, he continues, "so you want me to help your situation? To escape her?" "YES! I've been trapped there for weeks!" "Well, you've come to the right place, a hunter is exactly what you need. But..." a crooked smile crawls across his face, "there is the matter of price. We hunters don't work for free." What? This son-of-an-ogre wants you to pay him? After the town already has? "I'm sorry, but as I told you, I'm a slave. I have nothing to pay you with." your face has started to turn red, this time in anger. He misreads your emotions again, "No need to be shy, lass. You have plenty to pay with." he emphisizes his slimy request with a slow, obvious look up and down your body. You turn away, trying your best to calm yourself down before you explode. He relents for the moment, "Tell you what, as I said, I'm fasting at the moment, so we'd have to wait until later for such pleasantries. For now, why don't you tell me what you know about the wytch?" "I…actually I live there." He chuckles slightly and takes a step back. "Mysterious redhead with huge knockers, coming down from the mountain just to talk to me. I knew it!" as he moves, you catch the glint of a blade from behind the door! Combat instinct kicks in and you sidestep his his first lunge at you. You spin off your dodge in a fluid motion and rush forward to shove him off balance. Hopefully that will give yourself a chance to explain. This time however, your instincts fail you. You learned all your moves when you were much larger and much, much stronger - you push against him with all your weight and it does nothing! It's like pushing against a stone wall! The hunter takes the advantage to grab and slam you - painfully - against the door. You cry out in desperation as he raises his sword to finish you off, "I'm the Blackwolf!" He's still holding his sword up, ready to strike, but pauses, "What?" "The first hunter sent to kill the wytch was the Blackwolf. She caught me and changed me into a…into this." Despite the being pinned up against a wall, admitting this makes you drop your eyes in shame. The next thing you hear is raukous laughter as he lets you go. "Gods! You are Blackwolf! I see it in your eyes! Now this is a surprise! Got yourself in a fine mess." Your face burns bright red, "It wasn't my fault, she had a succubus that distracted me!" "Did you prepare against spells to protect yourself? Of course not. Never send a beast hunter to do a wytch hunter's job. Rushing in cock-first. Looks like that won't be an issue for you anymore! Nice tits though." "Yeah, thanks for reminding me," you"re suddenly feeling very self-conscious, and try to cover yourself by crossing your arms. When he finally finishes laughing, he puts away his sword and gets to business, "And I suppose you want my help to finish your job?" "No, it's not that!" you blurt out defensitely. "You want help with your…situation then?" He narrows his eyes, "Not that I wouldn't help if I could, but you know as well as I do that we don't dabble in magic. And I won't risk asking the wytch - too dangerous giving her a chance to speak. Best I can offer you now is a good fucking after we slay the wytch." You drop yor gaze again as a flood of conflicting emotions come over you. Your feelings fight between the Guild and , your old body and this new one, and whether you want to slap this hunter in the face or kick him in the balls more. He seems to mistake your thinking for disappointment, and pats you on the head, "Sorry Blackwolf, that was unkind. Tell you what, I can put in a good word for you with the Guild. Women aren't allowed to be hunters, but maybe they could find something for you. Maybe a cook or something? “First thing’s first though, we have to defeat the wytch. What can you tell me about her?"

passages.name: HunterAnswer
passages.content: Patience doesn’t seem to be one of his virtues, "Well then? What can you tell me about the wytch?" "You've probably already heard about her cabin. It's about halfway up the mountain in a little clearing. Very unassuming little place. The wytch is there most evenings, but she goes out most of the day." "I see, any guards or traps?" "No traps or guards that I know about. Well, like I said, there was a demon guarding the place, but at least I was able to destroy that.." "What's her key?" You look at him, slightly puzzled, "Key? I don't think she locks her doors." "No, her key phrase. The words she uses to cast her magic." "Oh of course! (embarrassed laugh) She says ‘My, what', before every spell" you shiver slightly thinking about the power of those words. "Good good, would you say is she a heavy sleeper?" "Fairly, yes. She doesn't usually wake up at night that I'm aware of." "Excellent, that helps immensely, she won't stand a chance." The dark tone of that last line emphasizes the grim reality of his task. You swallow a worried lump in your throat. Continue "Wait! I want to help, there's something about the wytch you need to know!" He's still eyeing you suspiciously, but stops trying to shoo you out. "Oh, and what might that be?" "I don't know how she does it, but she goes out to recharge her magic every day." "Every day? Busy girl." You pretend to not know what he means, "I'll take your word for it. She's mentioned it a few times, she goes to a place called Lorn's Peak. Like I said, I don't know what she does there, but if it's where she goes to 'recharge', that's probably where you'd find her at her weakest. Or at least, with the least amount of magic." As you speak, his mouth curls into a huge grin, "Now that is some helpful advice, ! Without magic a wytch is nothing but another frail little woman. You've done me and this town a great service." He approaches and touches you softly on the cheek, "We'll rid you of this scourge, I can promise. Now, I must finish preparing." His words make you want to roll your eyes again, but the softness of his touch, the concern in those deep blue eyes give you a little flutter in your chest. You nod, face turning red again, and show yourself out, quietly closing the door behind you. Gods, he bought it! You can't wait to tell . You make your way back out of the inn-and-tavern, head spinning with this new development. You've bought yourself a few more days! Leave "I want to help!" He looks stunned, "What?" "I want to help you defeat the wytch," you say again, more forcefully - more than a little annoyed at his reaction. "Listen , I appreciate the offer, but… Well, look at you! You can't even weigh seven stone. Can you even lift a proper sword? I get you want payback for what she's done to you, but you'd just be putting your life - and mine - in danger." "Ugh, I know, but thanks for reminding me," you roll your eyes extra hard at him for pointing out the obvious, "I know I'm no good in a fight, but I can help in other ways. I can signal when the coast is clear, let you in the cabin, help you hide, distract the wytch. Don't discount me just because I'm a woman!" That felt weird to say, but you say it with genuine passion. It has some effect, the Ending leans back and strokes his chin. "Good point…you could be useful." Your heart is pounding in your chest, this could be your best chance - your only chance. What if he doesn't accept? What if he still suspects you're the wytch? Why is he taking so long? Why does he have to look at my chest while thinking? You're nearly ready to blurt out another plea when he finally speaks. "Alright." "Yes! Thank you!" you're so relieved, jump up without even thinking about it, and give him a huge hug. It only strikes you how odd this is when you're already squeezed up against him. When did you become a hugger? You let go, face turning red again with embarrassment. The Ending seems likewise surprised (and a little embarrassed), "Ahem, yes, well, I'm glad to help, um…Blackwolf, ." As he readjusts his tunic…(and trousers, ew)…he goes on to explain his plan. "I'll finish preparations in days, on the th day, be in the cabin. Leave the door unlocked and help me prepare for when the wytch comes home. Don't worry about fighting, I'll handle that part so you don't get hurt." He approaches and touches you softly on the cheek, "We'll rid you of this scourge, I can promise. Now, I must finish preparing. days, be ready." His words make you want to roll your eyes again, but the softness of his touch, the concern in those deep blue eyes give you a little flutter in your chest. You nod, face turning red again, and show yourself out, quietly closing the door behind you. You make your way back out of the inn-and-tavern. Now it's only a matter of time: days left. Leave "Wait," you put a hand on the door to stop him, he waits. You use your finger to trace the wood-grain lines in the door and give your best pout. "I've heard about your exploits, Ending, and your impressive skills. Maybe you could…show me a few of your techniques?" He blinks a few times dumbly, before bursting out laughing. "Oh Blackwolf, what the gods did she do to you? Can you even hear yourself? You sound like a street harlot!" Your face turns bright red as he keeps laughing, "No! I just…learned to make the best of this situation!" That only makes him laugh harder. When he finally calms down, he wipes a tear from his eye. "Well, as much as I'd love to try out that magnificent ass you've got now, I'm still doing a fast for the Focusing ritual, so I'll have to decline." You're not sure if you're more disappointed by his reaction or by not getting sex. You shrug and do your best to seem unfazed, "Too bad. I can tell you, especially from a man's perspective, this is a hell of a body." He nods in agreement, "I'll admit, I'm impressed with the wytch's work. If the curse isn't lifted after I end her, I'll take you up on your offer." As you leave, he emphasizes his promise with a quick swat on your jiggly butt. Before you can reply the door closes again, his laughter still audible behind it. You use your finger to trace the wood-grain lines in the door and meet his gaze with a smile. "I've heard legends of hunters. and your impressive skills. Maybe you could…show me a few of your techniques?" "Ah ," he smiles as his eyes take a slow trip up and down your body, "you have no idea how hard it is to say no…but I can't. I'm in a fast, preparing for my hunt. I can't take any carnal pleasures…and you're about as carnal as they come." "Surely one quick bout wouldn't hurt?" "Sadly it would, that's how wytch magic works actually…" He pauses and runs a hand down the side of your arm to your waist, "…but, once this wytch business is settled, I'll be stopping back in town, if you're still interested." he reaches around behind and grabs a generous handful of your ass.cops a feel of your jiggly butt. You shiver slightly from his touch, "Mmmm, I look forward to your return then." You turn and walk away, but not before he has another go at your ass, smacking it as you walk away. Leave "You should leave." He draws back, looking more confused than anything else, "What?" "Leave this town, forget this job. Return the money, or take it for all I care." "Have you gone mad? I'm a hunter, this is what we do! We rid the world of monsters!" "I know, but what if they aren't all monsters? What if some just want to live in peace in their cabins in the woods? She hasn't even visited this town in weeks! What harm is she doing?" "How can you, of all people, say that? After what she's done to you!" You sigh. You wouldn't have imagined you'd be defending 's actions, but here you are. "I know, it's not like I'm happy about these changes, but you have to understand, she could have killed me, but she didn't. I think she only wanted me to see her point of view." His eyes soften for a moment, "I didn't even consider this..." You feel a wave of relief flowing over you, not to mention pride at diffusing the situation, "I know it's hard to consider after all you've seen as a hunter, but it's true. Not all the monsters we face are evil." "No." "No?" Your relief changes to worry as he closes his eyes, his face crunches together again in anger, "I hadn't considered that you'd actually fallen for her." "No...that's not it!" you take a step back, realizing how quickly and how bad the conversation just turned. "I've heard of this before, prisoners falling for their captors." His eyes soften for a moment, as he seems to consider your words. You feel a wave of hope flowing over you, "I know it's hard to consider after all you've seen as a hunter, but it's true. Not all the monsters you face are evil." "No." "No?" Your relief changes to worry as he closes his eyes, his face crunches together again in anger, "You're trying to help her. Or at least protect her." "No...that's not it!" you take a step back, realizing how quickly and how bad the conversation just turned. "I've heard of this before, victims falling for their captors." "That's sooo not what I'm talking about" you try to explain more but stop as his dark eyes shoot back open. They stare right into you, filled with anger and purpose. "You may not understand this now, but I'm fighting to save you too. Heed this warning, it's the only one you'll get. If you're not going to help me, stay out of my way. You may harbor misgivings or feelings about her, but they will lead to your ruin one way or another. And if you try to keep me from her, your fate will be the same as hers." The door closes quickly, before you can reply. Leaving you alone in the hallway with only a foreboding sense of dread. Leave "I don't think so. you say in an unsure, shaky tone. "Ok. I'll move as soon as I finish my ritual. If you think of anything else, do visit again." With that he ushers you back into the hall and closes the door. You make your way back out to the streets of Ridinghood, thinking over the consequences of what you've done. Leave "I'm sorry, there isn't much I can tell." He narrows his eyes in suspicion at you. "So you just stopped by to say you have nothing to tell me?" "No! I wanted you to see me, so you didn't mistake me for the Scarlet Wytch!" "Look, I'm busy, and I'm sure you are too with your...work. Good day then." You turn to walk away, but hear his voice once more from behind you, "Oh and one more thing. If you're not going to help me, stay out of my way. I know you're not the Scarlet Wytch, but if you try to keep me from her, your fate will be the same." The door closes quickly, before you can reply. Leaving you alone in the hallway with only a foreboding sense of dread. Leave

passages.name: DinnerHunterSeen
passages.content: You set out the dinner bowls and silverware as usual, while settles into her nightly routine of chatting, changes, and chow. You nod along, but your mind is still in town, thinking about the hunter you saw earlier. Suddenly she stops, "Are you alright ? You're quieter than usual." Shit, she noticed. Warn her so she doesn't get hurt! Warn her - after all, you might be a target too! Play it off, don't tell her about the hunter

passages.name: Play it off, don't tell her about the hunter
passages.content: The blood rushes to your face as you blush, ”Uh, nothing. I’m fine.” She side eyes you hard as you critically fail to play it off. ”Nice try, you’re hiding something. Come on, out with it! What did you see in town today?” You can feel her magic inside you, compelling you to tell the truth. Thankfully, she was being playful and it’s more of a nudge than a push - so you try a half-truth. ”I saw a new guy in town today, very tall, dark, and handsome.” She seems surprised for a moment, then smiles mischievously, ”Tall, dark, and handsome? A real triple threat. Why were you so shy about it, did you think he was cute?” ”Yes!” your face burns even redder as you answer. ”Well, well, well, who knew you had a type?” she laughs and ruffles your hair before heading upstairs for the night. ”I don't have a type!” you shout after her. You realize a moment later that you were under a truth compulsion when you answered yes…is he your “type” afterall? You don’t get long to think about it as calls you from upstairs, ”If you’re done dreaming, there’s a tall, red, and hot one waiting for you up here.” Upstairs You shove the thoughts from your mind as you get on with the dishes. Go upstairs to sleep

passages.name: Warn her about hunter
passages.content: "There's a new hunter in town!" you blurt out, not sure how to put it any more delicately. She stops, the smile drifting away from her face. "Oh, well that is some big news." "I have a little more information on the hunter." Her eyes light up, looking as if you'd offered her some juicy gossip, "Well then, let's talk!" "Actually, I know him." "You what!? Well this is a little awkward. Not to be rude to an old friend of yours, but is he any good?" You sigh and bite your lip, unhappy to deliver the bad news, "He's not a friend, but he is good. His nickname is 'The Ending', he's...a specialist in hauntings and wytches." She rubs the bridge of her nose in frustration "Damn it. I was hoping that if they lost one hunter, the village might just give up on sending another. I didn't know how dead-set they were on it. Honestly, I haven't even been to town in ages, I usually just send…" she looks up at you suddenly, turning white in concern, "Wait, did he see you?" "Um…kind of. I uh…talked to him." "I don't think so, but I did follow him a little." "What? , don't you know how dangerous that is? He's hunting a red-haired wytch! Have you looked at yourself recently?" "I was careful. Besides, I just might know a thing or two about hunters." "Well, that's a…very good point actually," she acknowledges, relaxing a little crossing her arms, "but still, a hunter is dangerous, and a prepared one…that's deadly. We have to take this very seriously, ." "He's doing something called a "Focusing" ritual. It's an old hunter ritual of fasting and meditation, designed to protect one against magic. I didn't put much faith in it, though in hindsight, I probably should have." Her eyes widen at the news, "That's…brilliant actually, you wouldn't be immune, but fasting would save up your magic, and meditation would focus your mind. Casting a spell on him would be very difficult. Terrifying, but brilliant." "It's also the one bit of good news. The ritual takes quite a while. We have a little time before he's ready. , by my guess."

passages.name: DinnerHunterSeenDecision
passages.content:
You lay awake in bed, heavy thoughts running through your mind.

Another hunter! What does this mean?

Did the town give up on the Blackwolf? You hate to admit it to yourself, but you failed your task by nearly every measure. But why are they still hunting ? She doesn't seem to bother the town. Hell she hasn't even been there since she sends…

Your eyes shoot wide open. Oh shit, you go all the time, do they think you're the Scarlet Wytch now?

What to do?

Help …you've come to care about her and don't want to see her hurt.

Help , not for her sake, but because the hunter is probably after you too.

Help the hunter, this might be your best chance to escape!

passages.name: hunterchoices
passages.content:
You should run (without me)!

We should run (together)!

Maybe I can reason with him.

I have an idea, maybe I can buy a little more time.

Hunters don't give up. I think we have to fight.

passages.name: FightDecision
passages.content:
You stare at the ceiling in the dark. Despite how tired you are, the swirling thoughts in your head keep you from sleeping.

Life is so strange. You've had many sleepless nights before a hunt. Preparing yourself to attack some monster somewhere…but now you're on the other side, wide awake and worrying about a hunter coming after you.

Fate, it seems, is not without a sense of irony.

You wonder if any of the creatures you'd hunted ever felt this way. Hunters go after creatures bigger and stronger than humans, so you'd never really thought they had reason to be scared…but then, you defeated all of them.

Well, almost all of them. You look over at , gently breathing in her sleep, the covers slowly rising and falling over her voluminous chest.

You don't look at her, or monsters the same way anymore. Hell, you don't look at yourself the same way anymore. Body changes aside, how do you feel about your old profession? Would you want to go back to being a hunter?

Without hesitation.

Maybe, but I think I'd be more carful about my targets.

No, that's not my life anymore.

passages.name: HunterAttack
passages.content: As you go about your business, a sudden wave of fear washes over you. Your trained senses noticing a change. The birds have stopped singing outside. Something is out there. "Damnit, you said you'd be back," you swear at under your breath. You jump into action, grabbing items to defend yourself. You probably look a little silly, dual-wielding a broom and a kitchen knife, but beggars can't be choosers. Not a moment too soon either! Seconds later, you hear a heartstopping slam as a large figure in black hunter's armor bursts through the front door wielding an ax. The black helmet on his head swivels, scanning the room; its narrow eye-slit stopping on you. "You?!? YOU'RE the Scarlet Wytch?!?" Before you have a chance to answer, he charges at you with a yell. Your combat senses are on overdrive, reading every movement. A charge was a bad choice in such close quarters, no time to build up momentum. You easily dodge low, out of the angle of his ax, and aim the broom handle up towards his neck. His own momentum makes it a solid hit, wrenching his head sideways and knocking his helmet clean off. Disoriented, he plows right into a bookshelf with a wall-shaking crash. You hear a groan from under the pile of books and wood as he scrambles back to his feet. Suddenly the air next to you shimmers and appears out of nowhere! "My what a shame you can't move!" The hunter freezes in place, still as a statue. "Told you I'd be here, ." says proudly, "you have NO idea how difficult an invisibility spell is!" "Men, so predictable." You only half listen, focused on the unmasked face of the hunter. Or rather, not the face of the hunter. He's wearing the right armor, but that's a local boy you “know" from towndefinitely not him. "Wait, that's not…" You don't have time to finish before noticing the glint of a blade from the top of the stairs. The real hunter must have climbed in the second floor window! He leaps from the mid-stairs and plows right into , laying her flat on the ground and knocking the wind out of her. He doesn't quite stick the landing and also stumbles to the ground. They're both on the floor, giving you a chance to act. Attack the hunter! Do Nothing Help the hunter!

You shut your eyes tight. You can't watch this, you can't even listen.

It's all too much. After weeks of imprisonment, changes, and too many emotions to process, you can't take it anymore. There is only one way to escape. And that's to run.

You bolt up from the floor and out the door, leaving it all behind you. You hear the sounds of shouting behind you, unsure whether your escape gave  a chance or not.

You keep running, driven by fear and excitement to be free again.

You eventually reach Ridinghood, panting and exhausted, and hide among the river docks for days. Terrified that the hunter or a vengefull wytch might show up looking for you.

Neither ever come.

Eventually the need for food and lodging force you to rejoin society.

You're even able to get a job at the local tavern, where you're endlessly popular for your cute looks and wild stories about wytches, hunters, and monsters.

With nothing better coming to mind, you even keep your new nickname, "Little Red of Ridinghood".

Months pass, but eventually your curiosity and guilt get the better of you, and you sneak back up the mountain to the old cabin.

It looks completely abandoned. Plants overgrowing the doors and windows, dust and dirt covering everything inside.

You search, but aren't able to find any clues about what happened after you left...until you pass by the fireplace.

A tiny glint catches your eye. There, hidden among the burnt logs is a small, silver locket. You open it up to find two pictures inside, carefully and beautifully painted. One of yourself, and another of Cassandra.

You hold the locket close and try to sniff back a mixture of happy and sad tears before heading back down the mountain to town.

The End

You take his warning to heart and leave quickly, rushing away without even looking at the cabin behind you.

You tell yourself you don't look back to keep from angering the hunter any further, but you know the real reason - you can't. Your eyes are already welling up with tears as you go. All you want to do right now is get away and forget everything that's happened here.

As you reach the treeline, you're practically in a run, and tears are flowing freely from the flood of emotion. Fear of the future, joy at escape, guilt about , it's all so overwhelming.

You don't know how long or far you've run until you reach a natural cliff on the mountain. Panting and out of breath, you pause and take a look at the view down the mountain.

The serene view helps you calm down. It feels like you can see the whole world spreading out before you. Nearby peaks, the little town of Ridinghood below, its river winding off into the distance where other towns surely lay. For a brief moment, you feel a glimmer of hope. All of it is there for you to explore and live again.

After packing your belongings - a few clothes given to you by  and a travel tankard of stew - you head out.

In the front yard, a breeze plays through the lush trees, swaying them against a blue sky. Sunlight filters softly through their branches to illuminate the small clearing that holds the little cabin. After the past few weeks, you've grown familiar with the sights and sounds, but they seem different now that you'll be leaving them behind. As you pass the chopping block, you run your hand along the smooth axe handle that you've gotten to know so well - and relish not having to pick it up ever again.

Reaching the edge of the yard, you take one last look at the little cabin. So serene and peaceful, it's hard to believe how such an unassuming little place completely changed your life. You give yourself a few final moments to remember the scene before turning down the mountain path.

As you walk, the mountain valley comes into view, it feels like you can see the whole world spreading out before you. Nearby peaks, the little town of Ridinghood below, its river winding off into the distance where other towns surely lay. All of it there for you to explore and live again.

passages.name: HuntressEnd
passages.content:
.hunterMark {display:none;}
#versionwarning {display:none;}

It was hard work, retraining yourself to fight with a much smaller and weaker body.

It was even harder work convincing the guild to admit you.

But no one could argue with the results.

Afterall, most supernatural creatures are bigger and stronger than humans anyway, so losing your strength wasn’t as daunting a problem as you'd worried. Hunting has always been more a game of wits than strength.

Your unique experience - not to mention a newfound empathy for magical creatures - made you one of the most famous and successful hunters in guild history. You even managed to change guild policy, helping to reform and make peace with magical creatures once thought irredeemable.

Sometimes, you still think back to your time in the cabin with . Even years later you still feel mixed emotions about it.

It was hard moving on, and you never managed to find a way to change back.

Of course now, years later, you’re not sure you would change back if you could. Those experiences made you who you are today, and you’re glad to remember them, even if bittersweetly. You even kept your old cloak from the cabin as a keepsake - which earned you your new hunter name:

Little Red Hood

The End
